---
title: "BinaryOutput_07_29_2025"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-14"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r Libraries, include=FALSE,echo=FALSE}
### PLEASE REFER TO NHANES-RMS-2024.08.06 for some additional code and checks that we deleted here for conciseness
### PLEASE REFER TO NHANES-RMS-2024.10.10 for some additional code and checks that we deleted here for conciseness

knitr::opts_chunk$set(include=FALSE, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE,message=FALSE)
options(prType='html')

#install.packages('nhanesA')
#install.packages('foreign')
#install.packages("xgboost")
#install.packages("caret")
#install.packages("dplyr")

library(xgboost)
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
library(foreign)
library(nhanesA)
library(tidyverse)
library(gridExtra)
library(broom)
require(rms)
library(ggplot2)
library(naniar)
#library(mosaic)
require(dplyr)
require(brms)
library(pROC)
library(AUC)
library(viridis)
library(Hmisc)
library(glmnet)
library(tidymodels)
library(data.table)
library(conflicted)
library(naniar)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)

conflict_prefer("rename","dplyr")
conflict_prefer("mutate","dplyr")
conflict_prefer("sum","base")
conflict_prefer("filter","dplyr")

library(conflicted)
conflicts_prefer(base::mean)

conflict_prefer_all("xgboost")
```

```{r Load DF.MASTER, echo=FALSE}

load('~/Documents/NHANES/df_full_2025.07.14.Rda')
```


```{r Making DF from DF_FULL}

f.yes1.no0 <- function(x) if_else(x=='Yes',1,if_else(x=='No',0,NA))
# Throwing out BP reading below 20
f.na.to.0 <- function(x) if_else(is.na(x),0,if_else(x<20,0,x))
f.na.or.low <- function(x) if_else(is.na(x) | x < 20,0,1)


df <- df_full %>% 
  mutate(
  ### Cr adjustment  
  #### Standard creatinine (mg/dL) = -0.016 + 0.978 X (NHANES 05-06 uncalibrated serum creatinine, mg/dL)
  LBXSCR = if_else(Year==2005, -0.016 + 0.978 * LBXSCR , LBXSCR)) %>% 

  rename(SEQN=SEQN,Age=RIDAGEYR,Gender=RIAGENDR,# age
              ## CBC
              wbc = LBXWBCSI, rbc = LBXRBCSI, hg = LBXHGB, mcv = LBXMCVSI, plt = LBXPLTSI, mpv = LBXMPSI, # WBC, RBC, Hg, MCV, PLT, MPV
              ## CMP
              alt = LBXSATSI, alb = LBXSAL, alp = LBXSAPSI, ast = LBXSASSI, # ALT, albumin, ALP, AST
              ## CMP
              bicarb = LBXSC3SI, bun = LBXSBU, chlor = LBXSCLSI, # Bicarb,  BUN, Chlor
              ## CMP
              cr = LBXSCR, # Creat
              ## CMP
              potas = LBXSKSI, #K
              ## CMP
              na = LBXSNASI, tbili = LBXSTB, cal = LBXSCA, tprot = LBXSTP, # Na, TBili, Cal, TProt
              fpg = LBXGLU, #fpg
        
              ## Vital Signs
              pulse = BPXPLS, reg.iregg = BPXPULS, sbp = BPXSY, dbp = BPXDI, 
              ## Weight Survey
             
              a1c = LBXGH, #glycohemoglobin
              sample.ogtt.weight = WTSOG2YR, two = LBXGLT,  # subsample weight, two hour glucose (mg/dL),
              dm.told = DIQ010, 
              Weight = BMXWT,
              Height.Stand = BMXHT,
              Arm.Circ = BMXARMC,
              Waist.Circ = BMXWAIST) %>% 

  rename(
    Seg.Neut.perc=LBXNEPCT,
    eosin.perc=LBXEOPCT, 
    Monocyte.perc=LBXMOPCT,
    RBC.Dist.Width=LBXRDW,
    Anemia.Treatment=MCQ053,
    Mean.Cell.Hemoglobin =LBXMCHSI) %>% 
  
  rename(
    TransfusionEver=MCQ092,
    MCHC=LBXMC,
    Told.Asthma = MCQ010,
    Baso.Perc=LBXBAPCT,
    BP.Ever.Told.High = BPQ020) %>% 
  
  rename(
    PovertyRatio=INDFMPIR,
    Race=RIDRETH1,
    Ppl.In.Household=DMDHHSIZ,
    Citizenship=DMDCITZN,
    EducationStatus=DMDEDUC2)

save(df,file="~/Documents/NHANES15-16//df.2025.07.14.Rda")

```



```{r Defining Column Functions}
 

f.align_columns <- function(ma, mb) {
  # force to plain character vectors
  ma_columns <- as.character(colnames(ma))
  mb_columns <- as.character(colnames(mb))
  
  # Identify the columns in MA but not in MB
  missing_columns <- setdiff(ma_columns, mb_columns)
  
  # Add missing columns to MB, filled with zeros
  for (col in missing_columns) {
    zero_col <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(mb), ncol = 1)
    colnames(zero_col) <- col
    mb <- cbind(mb, zero_col)
  }
  
  # Reorder columns in MB to match the order in MA
  mb <- mb[, ma_columns, drop = FALSE]
  
  return(mb)
}


```

```{r}
f.is.DM <- function(df.fx){
  dm = ifelse(
    is.na(df.fx$a1c) & is.na(df.fx$fpg) & is.na(df.fx$two), NA,
    ifelse(!is.na(df.fx$a1c) & df.fx$a1c >= 6.5, TRUE,
      ifelse(!is.na(df.fx$fpg) & df.fx$fpg >= 126, TRUE,
        ifelse(!is.na(df.fx$two) & df.fx$two >= 200, TRUE, FALSE)
      )
    )
  )
  return(dm)
}

```


```{r FILTER ON SOMETHING}

df.small <- df 

df.small$DM <- as.factor(f.is.DM(df.small))

dir.string <-"~/Documents/NHANES/"
date.string <- "2025.07.29-BinaryProbs"

file.office.train <- paste0(dir.string,"NHANES-office-train-layered_", date.string,".Rda")
file.office.test <- paste0(dir.string,"NHANES-office-test-layered_",date.string,".Rda")
file.list.models <- paste0(dir.string,"NHANES-All-xgb.models_",date.string,".Rda")
file.list.prepped.data <- paste0(dir.string,"NHANES-All-prepped.data_",date.string,".Rda")

```



```{r Prep XGBOOST: Assign labels }
f.clean.data.for.xgboost <- function(df,
                                     year_col    = "Year",
                                     label_col   = "DM",
                                     weight_col  = "sample.ogtt.weight",
                                     twohr_col   = "two",
                                     na_threshold = 0.88){

  # Define pre_df_raw without filtering for two/a1c/fpg as it's not a label-generating set
  pre_df_raw   <- df %>% filter(.data[[year_col]] >= 1999, .data[[year_col]] <= 2004)

  train_df_raw <- df %>% filter(.data[[year_col]] >= 2005, .data[[year_col]] <= 2014) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[twohr_col]]), !is.na(a1c), !is.na(fpg)) # <--- ADD THIS FILTER HERE

  test_df_raw  <- df %>% filter(.data[[year_col]] >= 2015, .data[[year_col]] <= 2016)

  # Now, DM is defined on dataframes that are already guaranteed to have non-NA glucose/a1c
  train_df_with_DM <- train_df_raw
  train_df_with_DM$DM <- as.numeric(f.is.DM(train_df_with_DM))
  train_df_with_DM <- train_df_with_DM %>% filter(!is.na(DM))

  test_df_with_DM <- test_df_raw
  test_df_with_DM$DM <- as.numeric(f.is.DM(test_df_with_DM))

  # Extract labels (already numeric 0/1 here and non-NA by design)
  labels_train  <- train_df_with_DM[[label_col]]
  labels_test   <- test_df_with_DM[[label_col]]

  # Extract other metadata/outcome variables from the DM-defined dataframes
  year_train    <- train_df_with_DM[[year_col]]
  year_test     <- test_df_with_DM[[year_col]]
  two_train     <- train_df_with_DM[[twohr_col]]
  two_test      <- test_df_with_DM[[twohr_col]]
  weight_train  <- train_df_with_DM[[weight_col]]
  weight_test   <- test_df_with_DM[[weight_col]]


  # Define columns that are NOT features for the model but ARE essential metadata/outcome variables.
  non_model_features_but_keep_in_df <- c("SEQN", year_col, label_col, weight_col, twohr_col)

  # Determine variables to keep based on the filtered train_df for features
  keep_vars_for_train_test_features <- names(train_df_with_DM)[vapply(train_df_with_DM, function(x) mean(!is.na(x)), numeric(1)) >= na_threshold]
  keep_vars_for_pre_features <- names(pre_df_raw)

  final_model_features_for_makeX <- unique(c(keep_vars_for_train_test_features, keep_vars_for_pre_features))
  final_model_features_for_makeX <- setdiff(final_model_features_for_makeX, non_model_features_but_keep_in_df)

  cols_for_output_df <- unique(c(final_model_features_for_makeX, non_model_features_but_keep_in_df))

  pre_df   <- pre_df_raw   %>% select(any_of(cols_for_output_df))
  train_df <- train_df_with_DM %>% select(any_of(cols_for_output_df))
  test_df  <- test_df_with_DM  %>% select(any_of(cols_for_output_df))

  list(
    office_pre             = pre_df,
    office_train           = train_df,
    office_test            = test_df,
    year.train             = year_train,
    year.test              = year_test,
    label.train            = labels_train, # These are guaranteed non-NA and 0/1
    label.test             = labels_test,  # These are guaranteed non-NA and 0/1
    train.two              = two_train,
    test.two               = two_test,
    weight.sample.train    = weight_train,
    weight.sample.test     = weight_test
  )
}
```

```{r}
f.just.prepare.data <- function(clean.output, config) {
  pre_data   <- clean.output$office_pre
  train_data <- clean.output$office_train
  test_data  <- clean.output$office_test

  get_model_features_only <- function(df_input) {
    # Ensure SEQN is included for tracking before it's temporarily renamed
    df_input %>%
      mutate(original_SEQN = SEQN) %>% # Add original_SEQN for tracking
      select(-any_of(c("SEQN", "DM", "sample.ogtt.weight", "two"))) # Exclude SEQN from features for makeX
  }

  # Apply the feature selection
  pre_processed   <- get_model_features_only(pre_data)
  train_processed <- get_model_features_only(train_data)
  test_processed  <- get_model_features_only(test_data)

  # Handle NAs and NaNs in numeric columns
  pre_processed <- pre_processed %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(., as.numeric(NA)))) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace(. , is.nan(.), as.numeric(NA))))

  train_processed <- train_processed %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(., as.numeric(NA)))) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace(. , is.nan(.), as.numeric(NA))))

  test_processed <- test_processed %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(., as.numeric(NA)))) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace(. , is.nan(.), as.numeric(NA))))

  drop_list_cols <- function(df) {
    df[, !sapply(df, is.list), drop = FALSE]
  }

  pre_clean   <- drop_list_cols(pre_processed)
  train_clean <- drop_list_cols(train_processed)
  test_clean  <- drop_list_cols(test_processed)

  # Training data preparation
  train_features_and_labels <- cbind(train_clean,
                                     label = clean.output$label.train,
                                     weight = clean.output$weight.sample.train)

  # Check if there are any complete cases for training
  if (nrow(train_features_and_labels[complete.cases(train_features_and_labels %>% select(-original_SEQN)), ]) == 0) {
    stop("No complete cases found for training data after feature NA removal. Adjust your NA thresholds or data.")
  }
  train_complete_cases <- train_features_and_labels[complete.cases(train_features_and_labels %>% select(-original_SEQN)), ]

  labels_train_filtered <- train_complete_cases$label
  weight_train_filtered <- train_complete_cases$weight
  train_seqns_filtered <- train_complete_cases$original_SEQN
  train_X_for_makeX <- train_complete_cases %>% select(-label, -weight, -original_SEQN)

  # Test data preparation
  test_features_and_labels <- cbind(test_clean,
                                    label = clean.output$label.test)

  test_seqns_filtered <- test_clean$original_SEQN
  test_X_for_makeX    <- test_clean   %>% select(-original_SEQN)
  labels_test_filtered <- clean.output$label.test
  
  pre_seqns_filtered <- pre_clean$original_SEQN
  pre_X_for_makeX    <- pre_clean %>% select(-original_SEQN)
  
  # Now, makeX on the cleaned-of-NA-rows dataframes.
  # Add checks for empty dataframes before makeX
  if (nrow(train_X_for_makeX) == 0) stop("train_X_for_makeX is empty, cannot proceed with makeX.")
  train_X <- makeX(as.data.frame(train_X_for_makeX))
  col_order <- colnames(train_X)

  # Handle potential empty pre_X and test_X after makeX or if input was empty
  if (nrow(pre_X_for_makeX) == 0) {
    pre_X <- matrix(0, nrow = 0, ncol = length(col_order))
    colnames(pre_X) <- col_order
  } else {
    pre_X   <- makeX(as.data.frame(pre_X_for_makeX))
    pre_X <- f.align_columns(train_X, pre_X)
    pre_X   <- pre_X[, col_order, drop = FALSE]
  }

  if (nrow(test_X_for_makeX) == 0) {
    test_X <- matrix(0, nrow = 0, ncol = length(col_order))
    colnames(test_X) <- col_order
  } else {
    test_X  <- makeX(as.data.frame(test_X_for_makeX))
    test_X <- f.align_columns(train_X, test_X)
    test_X <- test_X[, col_order, drop = FALSE]
  }

  # Create DMatrix objects, handling potentially empty ones for test/pre
  dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_X, label = labels_train_filtered)

if (nrow(test_X) > 0) {
    dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(data = test_X)
  } else {
    dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(data = matrix(0, nrow = 0, ncol = ncol(train_X)))
  }

  weight.sample.train.norm <- weight_train_filtered / sum(weight_train_filtered)

  list(
    pre_X = pre_X,
    train_X = train_X,
    dtrain = dtrain,
    test_X = test_X,
    dtest = dtest,
    weight.sample.train.norm = weight.sample.train.norm,
    labels.train.filtered = labels_train_filtered,
    labels.test.filtered = labels_test_filtered,
    train_seqns_filtered = train_seqns_filtered,
    test_seqns_filtered = test_seqns_filtered,
    pre_seqns_filtered = pre_seqns_filtered
  )
}
```

```{r Functions for running models}
f.run_xgb_model <- function(clean.output, config) {

  # Call f.just.prepare.data to get all prepped data
  prepped_data <- f.just.prepare.data(clean.output, config)

  dtrain <- prepped_data$dtrain
  dtest  <- prepped_data$dtest
  labels_train_filtered <- prepped_data$labels.train.filtered
  labels_test_filtered  <- prepped_data$labels.test.filtered
  weight_train_filtered <- prepped_data$weight.sample.train.norm # This is already normalized from f.just.prepare.data

  train_matrix           <- prepped_data$train_X
  # No need for manual filtering of labels/weights here, it's done in f.just.prepare.data

  # Add final check for labels (redundant if f.just.prepare.data is perfect, but good for safety)
  if (any(!is.finite(labels_train_filtered)) || any(is.na(labels_train_filtered))) {
    stop("ERROR: labels_train_filtered still contains NaN, Inf, or NA values before dtrain creation.")
  }


  w_caret            <- weight_train_filtered # Already normalized
  y_fac              <- factor(labels_train_filtered, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("zero","one"))
  tune_grid          <- expand.grid(
                          nrounds          = c(1500,2000),
                          eta              = c(0.01,0.05),
                          max_depth        = c(6,8),
                          subsample        = c(0.6,0.8),
                          colsample_bytree = c(0.6,0.8),
                          min_child_weight = 1,
                          gamma            = 0
                        )
  train_ctrl         <- caret::trainControl(
                          method          = "cv",
                          number          = 5,
                          classProbs      = TRUE,
                          summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
                          verboseIter     = FALSE
                        )
    print("Made it here")
  View(train_matrix)
  View(y_fac)
  View(train_ctrl)
  View(w_caret)
  print(typeof(train_matrix))
  xgb_tuned          <- caret::train(
                          x          = train_matrix, # Extract data from DMatrix for caret
                          y          = y_fac,
                          method     = "xgbTree",
                          tuneGrid   = tune_grid,
                          trControl  = train_ctrl,
                          metric     = "ROC",
                          weights    = w_caret,
                          verbose    = TRUE
                        )

  best_params        <- as.list(xgb_tuned$bestTune)
  best_params$objective <- "binary:logistic"
  xgb_model <- xgboost::xgb.train(
    params            = best_params,
    data              = dtrain,
    nrounds           = best_params$nrounds,
    watchlist         = list(train = dtrain),
    early_stopping_rounds = 50,
    verbose           = 1
  )

  pred_probs <- predict(xgb_model, dtest)

  list(
    model = xgb_model,
    preds = pred_probs,
    tuned = xgb_tuned,
    labels.train.filtered = labels_train_filtered, # Return these for potential later use
    labels.test.filtered = labels_test_filtered
  )
}


```

```{r}
conflict_prefer("arrange","plyr")
conflict_prefer("desc","plyr")
conflict_prefer("mosaic","plyr")
conflict_prefer("Matrix","plyr")
conflict_prefer("base","plyr")

```

```{r Loop for training and running models}
pre_idx <- which(df.small$Year >= 1999 & df.small$Year <= 2004)
train_idx <- which(df.small$Year >= 2005 & df.small$Year <= 2014)
test_idx  <- which(df.small$Year >= 2015 & df.small$Year <= 2016)

model_configs <- list(
  Full = list(
    mutate_expr = ~ .,
    pred_col = "Pred.Full"
  )
)

# Step 1: Clean and split initial dataframes
clean.output <- f.clean.data.for.xgboost(df.small)

# Step 2: Prepare data for XGBoost (feature matrices, filtered labels, and corresponding SEQNs)
# This step is now outside the model training loop to ensure consistent data prep.
prepped_data <- f.just.prepare.data(clean.output, model_configs$Full)


xgb_models <- list()

for (model_name in names(model_configs)) {
  cfg <- model_configs[[model_name]]
  
  # re‑prep each time so f.run_xgb_model() gets the full clean.output
  result        <- f.run_xgb_model(clean.output, cfg)
  prepped_data  <- f.just.prepare.data(clean.output, cfg)
  
  # store the fitted model
  xgb_models[[model_name]] <- result$model
  
  # pre‑period predictions
  dpre       <- xgb.DMatrix(data = prepped_data$pre_X)
  pre_preds  <- predict(result$model, dpre)
 temp_pre_preds <- tibble(
  SEQN = prepped_data$pre_seqns_filtered
) %>%
  mutate(
    # dynamically create column named in cfg$pred_col
    !!cfg$pred_col := pre_preds
  )
  
  # test‑period predictions
  temp_test_preds <- tibble(
    SEQN            = prepped_data$test_seqns_filtered,
    !!cfg$pred_col  := result$preds
  )
  
}

# Step 4: Construct the final `office_pre`, `office_train`, `office_test` dataframes
# Start with the original df.small filtered by year, then left_join the model results.

# Base dataframes (full columns from df.small before any makeX filtering)
office_pre_base   <- df.small %>% filter(Year >= 1999, Year <= 2004)
office_train_base <- df.small %>% filter(Year >= 2005, Year <= 2014)
office_test_base  <- df.small %>% filter(Year >= 2015, Year <= 2016)

temp_pre_preds <- temp_pre_preds %>%
  distinct(SEQN, .keep_all = TRUE)


# For office_pre (1999–2004): safe 1:1 join
office_pre <- office_pre_base %>%
  left_join(temp_pre_preds, by = "SEQN")

# For office_train (2005-2014): Join actual labels and weights used in training
train_model_results <- data.frame(
  SEQN = prepped_data$train_seqns_filtered,
  sample.ogtt.weight_model = prepped_data$weight.sample.train.norm
)

office_train <- office_train_base %>%
 left_join(train_model_results, by = "SEQN") %>%
  mutate(
    sample.ogtt.weight = if_else(
      !is.na(sample.ogtt.weight_model),
      sample.ogtt.weight_model,
      sample.ogtt.weight
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-sample.ogtt.weight_model)
 
test_model_results <- temp_test_preds

office_test <- office_test_base %>%
  left_join(test_model_results, by = "SEQN")

diq010_lookup <- df.small %>%
  select(SEQN, dm.told) %>%  # Use the correct name 'dm.told'
  distinct(SEQN, .keep_all = TRUE) # Ensure one row per participant

# Now, use a left_join to add the DIQ010 column back to each final data frame
office_pre <- office_pre %>%
  left_join(diq010_lookup, by = "SEQN")

office_train <- office_train %>%
  left_join(diq010_lookup, by = "SEQN")

office_test <- office_test %>%
  left_join(diq010_lookup, by = "SEQN")

# Step 5: Save the final dataframes
save(office_pre,   file = paste0(dir.string, "NHANES-office-pre_",   date.string, ".Rda"))
save(office_train, file = paste0(dir.string, "NHANES-office-train_", date.string, ".Rda"))
save(office_test,  file = paste0(dir.string, "NHANES-office-test_",  date.string, ".Rda"))

# Optional: Save models and prepped data if desired for debugging/reuse
save(xgb_models, file = file.list.models)
save(prepped_data, file = file.list.prepped.data) # This will save the output of f.just.prepare.data
```
